skill.name: readme
skill.description: Generates or updates comprehensive README.md documentation by inspecting project structure, setup requirements, environment variables, scripts, architecture, deployment, and troubleshooting guidance.
skill.summary: Create thorough project README documentation from repository context.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, and credential patterns, but the reviewed file is a markdown README-generation prompt with example commands rather than executable code. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill directs agents to inspect credential-related configuration and may cause sensitive names or operational commands to be written into public documentation if the output is not reviewed.

medium_findings.title: Broad Repository and Configuration Inspection
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to explore project structure, configuration files, database files, dependency files, scripts, CI/CD files, and deployment files before writing documentation. This is legitimate for README generation, but it increases exposure to sensitive repository details and requires output review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require broad file inspection, including configuration and deployment files. The context is documentation, so the risk is real but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Environment Documentation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The README template asks the agent to document environment variables and credential file locations, including database URLs, secret keys, Rails master keys, and encrypted credentials. These are placeholders and guidance, but generated output could expose sensitive operational details if copied from a real project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines clearly reference secret-related configuration and credential files. The examples use placeholders, so this is a controlled documentation risk rather than confirmed credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Example Shell Commands Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many shell commands, sudo usage, and command substitutions. Reviewed instances are inside markdown examples for README content, not executable scripts bundled with the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear in fenced markdown examples and there are no separate executable files in the skill. They can still lead to unsafe documentation if copied without review, but they are not direct execution paths.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs and Network References Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs and network-related values are source metadata, deployment platform examples, or local connection examples. No evidence found of network requests or data exfiltration code in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain metadata and placeholder environment values rather than code that sends data. The single-file skill contains instructions only.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation Examples Include Privileged and Destructive Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes README examples with sudo package installation, service restart, and removal of credential or dependency files. These are common troubleshooting examples, but published READMEs should make their scope and consequences clear.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in README examples and include sudo and rm usage. They are not executed by the skill itself, so the concern is user safety in generated documentation.

content.value_statement: Project README files often miss setup, architecture, and deployment details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to inspect a repository and write complete developer documentation.
actual_capabilities: Creates or updates a README.md file for a software project.
Guides repository inspection for frameworks, dependencies, scripts, databases, and deployment files.
Produces sections for overview, tech stack, prerequisites, local setup, architecture, environment variables, and troubleshooting.
Adapts deployment guidance for common platforms such as Docker, Heroku, Fly.io, Render, and VPS hosts.
Adds command examples, tables, and a table of contents for long README files.
Writes the final documentation directly to README.md in the project root.
limitations: It is optimized for README files, not full documentation sites or API reference generation.
It may include generic deployment guidance when the repository has no deployment configuration.
It needs repository access to produce accurate setup and architecture details.
Users should review secret names and credential instructions before publishing generated documentation.

use_cases.title: Document a New Repository
use_cases.description: Create a complete README that explains what the project does, how to install it, and how to run it locally.

use_cases.title: Improve Team Onboarding
use_cases.description: Update sparse documentation with prerequisites, scripts, environment variables, architecture notes, and troubleshooting steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare Deployment Notes
use_cases.description: Add deployment sections based on detected platform files, database setup, migrations, and operational commands.

prompt_templates.title: Create a README
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a missing README.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete README.md for this project. Include setup, usage, project structure, and troubleshooting.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a README that already exists.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the existing README.md and update it with missing setup, architecture, environment, script, and deployment details.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Onboarding
prompt_templates.scenario: Make local development easier for new contributors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite the README.md for new developer onboarding. Emphasize prerequisites, first run steps, common errors, and expected commands.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Deployment Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the repository for deployment configuration and update README.md with platform-specific deployment, environment, database, and rollback guidance.

output_examples.input: Create a README for a Rails project with Docker and PostgreSQL.
output_examples.output: A structured README with overview, tech stack, prerequisites, Docker setup, database setup, development commands, environment variables, deployment notes, and troubleshooting.

output_examples.input: Improve our README for new contributors.
output_examples.output: An onboarding-focused README with clear installation steps, script descriptions, architecture notes, testing instructions, and common problem fixes.
A deployment section that reflects detected platform files and explains build commands, migrations, environment variables, and service restart steps.

best_practices: Review the generated README before publishing to remove private paths, credentials, or internal service names.
Run the documented setup commands in a clean environment to verify accuracy.
Keep generated deployment guidance aligned with the actual hosting platform and team procedures.
anti_patterns: Do not publish real secrets, tokens, private keys, or production connection strings in README.md.
Do not accept generic deployment commands without checking the repository and hosting platform.
Do not use this skill as a replacement for dedicated API reference or security documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill execute setup or deployment commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides documentation guidance and example commands for README content.

faq.question: Can it update an existing README?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect the current README and add missing setup, architecture, deployment, and troubleshooting sections.

faq.question: Does it support non-Rails projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks the agent to identify project files and adapt documentation to the detected framework and tooling.

faq.question: Why is this skill marked medium risk?
faq.answer: It asks the agent to inspect configuration and credential-related files, so generated documentation needs careful review before publishing.

faq.question: Will it include a table of contents?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions request a linked table of contents for long README files.

faq.question: Can it document deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. It detects common deployment files and writes deployment guidance for platforms such as Docker, Heroku, Fly.io, Render, and VPS hosts.
